questions.text: Who is the narrator of the story 'The Snake and the Mirror'?
questions.explanation: The story is told by a homeopath about a terrifying yet funny incident from his past.

questions.text: What time of day did the incident take place?
questions.explanation: He had finished his meal at a restaurant and returned to his room at about ten o’clock.

questions.text: What was the state of the doctor's room?
questions.explanation: He was just starting his practice and lived in a poor, un-electrified rented room.

questions.text: What did the doctor hear when he entered his room?
questions.explanation: He and the rats shared the room, and he heard their movement frequently.

questions.text: What was the doctor's financial condition at that time?
questions.explanation: He had about sixty rupees in his suitcase and very few clothes.

questions.text: What did the doctor do while sitting in front of the mirror?
options: He admired his own looks and made 'important' decisions
questions.explanation: Being a bachelor and a doctor, he felt he had to look handsome and made decisions like growing a thin moustache.

questions.text: What 'earth-shaking' decision did the doctor make while looking in the mirror?
options: To shave daily and grow a thin moustache
questions.explanation: He wanted to enhance his handsome appearance.

questions.text: What kind of woman did the doctor want to marry?
options: A woman doctor with plenty of money and a fat body
questions.explanation: He wanted a fat wife so that if he made a mistake and had to run away, she wouldn't be able to catch him.

questions.text: What happened suddenly while he was sitting on the chair?
options: A snake landed on his shoulder
questions.explanation: A rubber-like thing fell, and a cobra wriggled over the back of the chair and onto his shoulder.

questions.text: How did the doctor react when the snake was on his arm?
options: He sat like a stone image in flesh
questions.explanation: He didn't move or tremble because any sudden movement would make the snake strike.

questions.text: How far was the snake's head from the doctor's face?
questions.explanation: The snake's hood was spread out, and its head was very close to his face.

questions.text: What did the doctor think about in his moment of fear?
questions.explanation: He felt the presence of the Creator and realized he didn't even know what medicine to take if bitten.

questions.text: Why did the snake eventually leave the doctor?
options: It saw its reflection in the mirror
questions.explanation: The snake turned its head and looked into the mirror, seemingly fascinated by its own reflection.

questions.text: What did the doctor do as soon as the snake moved toward the mirror?
options: He ran out of the room
questions.explanation: He quietly got up, went through the door, and ran until he reached a friend's house.

questions.text: What did the doctor do at his friend's house?
options: He smeared oil over himself and took a bath
questions.explanation: He cleaned himself up to get over the terrifying experience.

questions.text: When the doctor returned to his room the next morning, what did he find?
options: Most of his things were stolen by a thief
questions.explanation: A thief had removed most of his things during the night.

questions.text: What was the one thing the thief had left behind?
questions.explanation: The thief left his dirty vest as a final 'insult' to the doctor's cleanliness.

questions.text: Did the doctor ever see the snake again?
questions.explanation: The story concludes by saying he never saw it again.

questions.text: Was the doctor's wife actually fat, as he had wished?
options: No, she was a thin, 'reedy' person
questions.explanation: In reality, his wife was a thin person with the 'gift of a sprinter'.

questions.text: The story 'The Snake and the Mirror' is written by:
questions.explanation: He is a famous Malayalam writer whose works are known for their humor.

questions.text: The doctor's room was full of rats because:
options: The room was unhygienic and old
questions.explanation: It was an old, outer room with much traffic of rats.

questions.text: What was the 'dull thud' the doctor heard?
options: A fat snake falling to the ground
questions.explanation: It was the sound of the snake falling from the roof onto his chair.

questions.text: What medicine did the doctor think of when the snake coiled around his arm?
options: None (He realized he had no medicines)
questions.explanation: In his fear, he realized there were no medicines in the room for a snake bite.

questions.text: What kind of snake was it?
questions.explanation: The text refers to it as a cobra with a spread hood.

questions.text: How did the doctor describe his state while sitting on the chair?
options: A stone image in the flesh
questions.explanation: He was physically frozen but his mind was very active.

questions.text: The doctor felt he was a 'poor, foolish and ________' doctor.
questions.explanation: He laughed at himself in his moment of danger.

questions.text: What was the 'vile' thing the thief left behind?
questions.explanation: The doctor jokingly called his own dirty vest 'vile' because the thief rejected it.

questions.text: The snake's interest in the mirror shows that it was:
questions.explanation: The story humorously suggests the snake was admiring itself.

questions.text: What book was the doctor reading before the incident?
questions.explanation: He opened the box and took out the book 'Materia Medica' to read.

questions.text: The room had no electricity, so the doctor used a:
questions.explanation: He lighted a kerosene lamp on the table.

questions.text: The doctor's decision to always keep an 'attractive smile' on his face was to:
questions.explanation: It was another one of his 'important' decisions based on vanity.

questions.text: What does 'meagre' mean?
questions.explanation: The doctor had very small (meagre) earnings from his practice.

questions.text: Who had 'taken away' the rats?
questions.explanation: The doctor joked that the thief was so thorough that even the rats had been cleaned out.

questions.text: Which part of the doctor's body did the snake coil around?
options: His left arm above the elbow
questions.explanation: It coiled around his left arm, making it heavy and painful.

questions.text: What was the snake doing after it reached the table?
questions.explanation: It wanted to enjoy its reflection at closer quarters.

questions.text: The doctor felt the 'Presence of God' in the:
questions.explanation: In his extreme danger, he felt spiritual in the pitch darkness.

questions.text: The noise from above stopped after:
questions.explanation: The 'rats' noise stopped because it was actually the snake moving.

questions.text: What was the doctor's friend's reaction to the story?
questions.explanation: The story is presented as a humorous anecdote shared among friends.

questions.text: Which of these words describes the doctor's initial character?
questions.explanation: His excessive focus on his looks in the mirror shows his vanity.

questions.text: The 'gift of a sprinter' refers to which character?
questions.explanation: It describes his wife's ability to run fast because she was very thin.
